sections.title: Phrasal verbs
items: **call back** — ring again on the phone
**fill in** — add information in the spaces on a form, etc
**hang up** — put the receiver down to end a phone call
**log off** — disconnect from the Internet/a website
**log on (to)** — connect to the Internet/a website
**print out** — make a paper copy of sth on a computer

exercises.title: Topic vocabulary (gap fill)
exercises.instructions: Complete the sentences with words from the box.
exercises.studentInstruction: Complete the sentences with words from the box.

items.sentence: I saved the ...... in a folder called 'English homework'.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is digital document, so the correct answer is "file".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Check whether the noun is specific, countable, or mentioned for the first time.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Articles show whether a noun is specific (the), new and countable (a/an), or general.

items.sentence: Please don't ...... the teacher while she's explaining the task.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is stop someone speaking, so the correct answer is "interrupt".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Check whether the noun is specific, countable, or mentioned for the first time.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Articles show whether a noun is specific (the), new and countable (a/an), or general.

items.sentence: There was an ...... at the airport about a cancelled flight.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is public message, so the correct answer is "announcement".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Check whether the noun is specific, countable, or mentioned for the first time.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Articles show whether a noun is specific (the), new and countable (a/an), or general.

items.sentence: Which ...... usually shows nature documentaries?

explanationContent.whyCorrect: TV channel shows documentaries.
explanationContent.commonMistake: Compare your answer with the grammar or vocabulary clue in the sentence — tense, form, or collocation may be wrong.
explanationContent.usefulTip: Read the full sentence with your answer inserted — if it sounds wrong, check tense, preposition, or word form.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Relative clauses add detail about a noun — choose the pronoun that matches the antecedent.

items.sentence: His Scottish ...... is difficult for me to understand sometimes.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is way of speaking, so the correct answer is "accent".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Try saying the phrase aloud — the correct preposition is usually part of a fixed pattern.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Prepositions often form fixed patterns — learn them as chunks, not word by word.

items.sentence: You can download the application form from our ...... .

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is online, so the correct answer is "website".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Try saying the phrase aloud — the correct preposition is usually part of a fixed pattern.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Prepositions often form fixed patterns — learn them as chunks, not word by word.

items.sentence: The radio station will ...... the concert live tomorrow.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is transmit live, so the correct answer is "broadcast".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Check whether the noun is specific, countable, or mentioned for the first time.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Articles show whether a noun is specific (the), new and countable (a/an), or general.

items.sentence: I couldn't call you because my ...... was out of battery.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is portable phone, so the correct answer is "mobile phone".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Check the word order and particles — English often needs the full chunk "mobile phone", not just one word.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Prepositions often form fixed patterns — learn them as chunks, not word by word.

exercises.title: Circle the correct word
exercises.instructions: Choose the correct option in each sentence.
exercises.studentInstruction: Choose the correct option in each sentence.

items.originalSentence: Can you **pronounce / persuade** your surname slowly, please?
items.completedSentence: Can you pronounce your surname slowly, please?

explanationContent.whyCorrect: Only "pronounce" fits the meaning created by the words before and after the gap.
explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: "pronounce" collocates with the rest of the sentence. "persuade" would change the meaning or is the wrong register for this context.

wrongOptions.persuade: "persuade" does not match the clue in the rest of the sentence ("your surname slowly, please?").

explanationContent.usefulTip: When two words look similar, read the whole sentence — the clue is usually before or after the gap.

items.originalSentence: Don't **swear / whisper** during the interview; be polite.
items.completedSentence: Don't swear during the interview; be polite.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: Only "swear" fits the meaning created by the words before and after the gap.
explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: "swear" collocates with the rest of the sentence. "whisper" would change the meaning or is the wrong register for this context.

wrongOptions.whisper: "whisper" does not match the clue in the rest of the sentence ("during the interview; be polite.").

explanationContent.usefulTip: When two words look similar, read the whole sentence — the clue is usually before or after the gap.

items.originalSentence: I need to **type / ring** my password carefully.
items.completedSentence: I need to type my password carefully.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: Only "type" fits the meaning created by the words before and after the gap.
explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: "type" collocates with the rest of the sentence. "ring" would change the meaning or is the wrong register for this context.

wrongOptions.ring: "ring" does not match the clue in the rest of the sentence ("my password carefully.").

explanationContent.usefulTip: When two words look similar, read the whole sentence — the clue is usually before or after the gap.

items.sentenceAfter: for a message to a close friend.
items.originalSentence: This e-mail sounds too **formal / clear** for a message to a close friend.
items.completedSentence: This e-mail sounds too formal for a message to a close friend.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: Only "formal" fits the meaning created by the words before and after the gap.
explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: "formal" collocates with the rest of the sentence. "clear" would change the meaning or is the wrong register for this context.

wrongOptions.clear: "clear" does not match the clue in the rest of the sentence ("for a message to a close friend.").

explanationContent.usefulTip: When two words look similar, read the whole sentence — the clue is usually before or after the gap.

items.sentenceAfter: said there might be snow tonight.
items.originalSentence: The weather **report / request** said there might be snow tonight.
items.completedSentence: The weather report said there might be snow tonight.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: Only "report" fits the meaning created by the words before and after the gap.
explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: "report" collocates with the rest of the sentence. "request" would change the meaning or is the wrong register for this context.

wrongOptions.request: "request" does not match the clue in the rest of the sentence ("said there might be snow tonight.").

explanationContent.usefulTip: When two words look similar, read the whole sentence — the clue is usually before or after the gap.

items.sentenceAfter: the blue button to upload the image.
items.originalSentence: You need to **click / pause** the blue button to upload the image.
items.completedSentence: You need to click the blue button to upload the image.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: Only "click" fits the meaning created by the words before and after the gap.
explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: "click" collocates with the rest of the sentence. "pause" would change the meaning or is the wrong register for this context.

wrongOptions.pause: "pause" does not match the clue in the rest of the sentence ("the blue button to upload the image.").

explanationContent.usefulTip: When two words look similar, read the whole sentence — the clue is usually before or after the gap.

exercises.title: Phrasal verbs (complete the gaps)
exercises.instructions: Write the correct phrasal verb in each gap.

items.sentence: I'm busy now, but I'll ...... in ten minutes.

items.sentence: Please don't ...... before I give you the reference number.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is end a call, so the correct answer is "hang up".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Past simple is wrong when the sentence links past to now (for, since, already, yet, ever).
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Present perfect links a past action to the present or unfinished time.

exercises.title: Prepositional phrases

explanationContent.grammarFocus: Past simple is used for finished actions at a specific past time.

exercises.title: Word formation
exercises.instructions: Complete each sentence with the correct form of the word in capitals.
exercises.studentInstruction: Complete each sentence with the correct form of the word in capitals.

items.sentence: I'll ...... call you when I arrive. (CERTAIN)

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is adverb from CERTAIN, so the correct answer is "certainly".

wrongOptions.certain: The root CERTAIN cannot fill the gap alone — you need a derived form like "certainly".

explanationContent.commonMistake: Check the suffix and word class — the gap needs the form of CERTAIN that fits grammatically, not just the root.
explanationContent.usefulTip: Identify the word class needed (noun, adjective, adverb) before choosing the suffix from the root in capitals.
explanationContent.wordFormation: CERTAIN → certainly: Use the -ing form for an activity or gerund.
explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: "certainly" is the word-formation answer from the root CERTAIN in this context.

items.sentence: Modern phones have changed ...... completely. (COMMUNICATE)

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is noun from COMMUNICATE, so the correct answer is "communication".

wrongOptions.communicate: The root COMMUNICATE cannot fill the gap alone — you need a derived form like "communication".

explanationContent.commonMistake: Check the suffix and word class — the gap needs the form of COMMUNICATE that fits grammatically, not just the root.
explanationContent.usefulTip: Identify the word class needed (noun, adjective, adverb) before choosing the suffix from the root in capitals.
explanationContent.wordFormation: COMMUNICATE → communication: Change the word class to a noun with the right suffix.
explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: "communication" is the word-formation answer from the root COMMUNICATE in this context.

items.sentence: There was a problem with the Internet ...... . (CONNECT)

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is noun from CONNECT, so the correct answer is "connection".

wrongOptions.connect: The root CONNECT cannot fill the gap alone — you need a derived form like "connection".

explanationContent.commonMistake: Check the suffix and word class — the gap needs the form of CONNECT that fits grammatically, not just the root.
explanationContent.usefulTip: Identify the word class needed (noun, adjective, adverb) before choosing the suffix from the root in capitals.
explanationContent.wordFormation: CONNECT → connection: Change the word class to a noun with the right suffix.
explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: "connection" is the word-formation answer from the root CONNECT in this context.

items.sentence: The ...... of the package took three days. (DELIVER)

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is noun from DELIVER, so the correct answer is "delivery".

wrongOptions.deliver: The root DELIVER cannot fill the gap alone — you need a derived form like "delivery".

explanationContent.commonMistake: Check the suffix and word class — the gap needs the form of DELIVER that fits grammatically, not just the root.
explanationContent.usefulTip: Identify the word class needed (noun, adjective, adverb) before choosing the suffix from the root in capitals.
explanationContent.wordFormation: DELIVER → delivery: Change the word class to a noun with the right suffix.
explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: "delivery" is the word-formation answer from the root DELIVER in this context.

wrongOptions.express: The root EXPRESS cannot fill the gap alone — you need a derived form like "expression".

wrongOptions.inform: The root INFORM cannot fill the gap alone — you need a derived form like "informative".

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The context points to "call back" — driving now.

exercises.title: Mixed revision
exercises.instructions: Complete each sentence with one suitable word.
exercises.studentInstruction: Complete each sentence with one suitable word.

items.sentence: Please send the photos by ...... when you get home.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is method, so the correct answer is "e-mail".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Check whether the noun is specific, countable, or mentioned for the first time.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Articles show whether a noun is specific (the), new and countable (a/an), or general.

items.sentence: I need to ...... up now because my train is arriving.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is end a call, so the correct answer is "hang".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Compare your answer with the grammar or vocabulary clue in the sentence — tense, form, or collocation may be wrong.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: "hang" is the only word or phrase that fits both grammar and meaning in: "I need to ...... up now because my train is arriving.".

exercises.title: Key word transformation
exercises.instructions: Complete the second sentence using a maximum of four words, so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence.
